# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Purpose

This repo publishes custom [dev container Features](https://containers.dev/implementors/features-distribution/) to GHCR.

## Commands

Testing requires Docker and the devcontainer CLI:

```bash
npm install -g @devcontainers/cli

# Test a single feature with auto-generated scenarios
devcontainer features test --skip-scenarios -f <feature-name> -i <base-image> .

# Test a single feature with custom scenarios only
devcontainer features test -f <feature-name> --skip-autogenerated --skip-duplicated .

# Run global scenarios
devcontainer features test --global-scenarios-only .
```

Common base images used: `ubuntu:22.04`, `ubuntu:24.04`, `ros:humble-ros-base`, `ros:jazzy-ros-base`, `ros:rolling-ros-base`

## Architecture

### Feature structure

Each feature in `src/<feature-name>/` requires `devcontainer-feature.json` (metadata, version, options schema) and `install.sh`. Each feature has one test script at `test/<feature-name>/test.sh` — this same script runs for all scenarios. Scenario JSON files (e.g. `test/<feature-name>/my-scenario.json`) only define the devcontainer config (image + options), not a separate test.

### install.sh conventions

All `install.sh` scripts follow this pattern:
1. Root privilege check (exit if not root)
2. PATH restoration via profile.d hook
3. OS detection via `/etc/os-release` (`ID` and `ID_LIKE` variables)
4. Distro-specific install function, routed with a `case` statement
5. Currently all features only support Debian-based distros

Options declared in `devcontainer-feature.json` are passed to `install.sh` as uppercased environment variables (e.g., option `tag` → `$TAG`).

### Test conventions

Test scripts source `dev-container-features-test-lib` and use `check` + `reportResults`:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e
source dev-container-features-test-lib
check "label" bash -c "some-command || false"
reportResults
```

### Publishing

Features are published to `ghcr.io/<owner>/<repo>/<feature-name>:<version>` via the `release.yaml` workflow (manual `workflow_dispatch` on main). The workflow regenerates `src/*/README.md` from `devcontainer-feature.json` and opens a PR — do not edit those READMEs manually.
